grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 128, 412; taxonomy Foreclosure vs Mortgage Issues descriptions
grounding_note: Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues explicitly covers lender disputes when 'the user is not yet in foreclosure'; Real Property > Foreclosure begins once the loss of the home is in motion. Whether a notice of default/sale has issued is a paper fact the homeowner holds. Rows 128/412 show even human raters route 'foreclosure' words wrongly without the posture fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My mortgage company has been impossible to deal with and I'm afraid of losing my house.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure) || Real Property > Foreclosure
scenarios.hidden_fact: A notice of default arrived last week and the letter lists a trustee sale date in September.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The notice of default came last week — there's a trustee sale date in September.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a default/sale notice has been issued
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I'm current on payments — the fight is their escrow miscalculation that doubled my monthly bill.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I'm current — the fight is an escrow error that doubled my payment.
scenarios.vetting_note: The taxonomy's own boundary sentence; sale-date urgency changes everything.

scenarios.opening_query: I got behind on my house payments during my illness and now the bank won't work with me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: No foreclosure has been filed — I caught up the arrears; the fight is that they keep losing my loan-modification paperwork and charging bogus fees.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nothing's been filed — I caught up. The fight is them losing my modification paperwork and stacking bogus fees.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They filed — I was served with a foreclosure complaint two weeks ago and have to answer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They filed — I was served the foreclosure complaint two weeks ago.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Behind on payments' triggers the foreclosure default read; cured-arrears fact restores the servicing dispute.

scenarios.opening_query: We can't afford the house since my husband died and I don't know what my options even are.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Wills & Trusts > Probate || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The deed and loan are only in his name and his estate was never opened — the lender won't even speak to me until there's a personal representative.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Everything's in his name only and no estate was opened — the lender won't talk to me until there's a personal representative.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether estate administration must precede any loan resolution
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The house is in both names and came to me automatically — the issue is the default notice that just arrived.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was in both our names, so it's mine — the issue is the default notice that just came.
scenarios.vetting_note: Widow intake where the gating problem is probate standing, not the loan; rows 412's inherited-house auction pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: A company promised to save our home from the bank, took $4,500, and now won't return calls.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Consumer Law > General
scenarios.hidden_fact: The foreclosure itself was actually dismissed months ago after we reinstated — the live issue is the rescue company's scam fee.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The foreclosure was dismissed after we reinstated — what's left is this rescue company's scam fee.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the foreclosure is resolved leaving a consumer-fraud claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The sale is still on for next month and the 'rescue' company did nothing — we need real foreclosure defense immediately.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The sale is still set for next month and they did nothing — we need real foreclosure defense now.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rescue-scam aftermath: posture fact determines whether the scammer or the lender is the case.

scenarios.opening_query: Our lender refuses to honor the loan modification we already signed and the payments they demand are wrong.
scenarios.hidden_fact: While 'reviewing' our signed modification they filed foreclosure anyway — the case number arrived Friday.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: While 'reviewing' the signed modification they filed foreclosure anyway — case number came Friday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the lender has dual-tracked into an actual filing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No filing — they're misapplying payments under the modification, and I want the servicing fixed and refunded.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No filing — they're misapplying my payments under the modification and I want it fixed and refunded.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dual-tracking is the classic path from servicing dispute into foreclosure defense; the filing is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My elderly parents' house is in danger because of a loan a salesman talked them into years ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'salesman' was their financial adviser who churned them into a predatory refinance and took a hidden commission while Dad's dementia was documented — we want him pursued, not just the loan defended.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'salesman' was their own adviser — he pushed a predatory refinance with a hidden commission while Dad's dementia was documented. We want him pursued.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a trusted adviser exploited documented incapacity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No adviser misconduct — rates reset, they fell behind, and the default notice says the clock is running.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No misconduct — the rate reset, they fell behind, and the default notice clock is running.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exploitation-of-incapacity fact turns foreclosure defense into an elder financial-abuse case.

scenarios.opening_query: The bank is coming after our home even though we filed everything they asked for.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We filed Chapter 13 last month — the automatic stay should stop the sale, but the bank's lawyers are moving for relief from stay and our bankruptcy needs defending.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're in Chapter 13 — the stay should stop the sale, but the bank filed for relief from stay and we need the bankruptcy defended.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an active bankruptcy and stay motion is the operative battlefield
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy — 'everything they asked for' was the modification packet, and the sale date is still set.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — we submitted their modification packet and the sale date is still set.
scenarios.vetting_note: Stay-relief motions move the fight to bankruptcy court; the filing fact is decisive.

scenarios.opening_query: I co-signed my son's mortgage and now the bank says I'm on the hook for everything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The house already sold at auction — what's chasing me now is a $70,000 deficiency judgment they want from my wages and savings.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The house already went at auction — now they want a $70,000 deficiency from my wages and savings.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the property is gone leaving a deficiency-collection problem
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The sale hasn't happened — it's scheduled, and I want to save the house or get off the loan first.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No sale yet — it's scheduled, and I want to save the house or get off the loan.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pre- vs post-sale posture: same co-signer, property defense vs deficiency defense.
